steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains the mechanism: water evaporates from the crust $\Rightarrow$ voids form $\Rightarrow$ oil fills the voids $\Rightarrow$ fat content rises. The sentence to complete starts "the less moisture a food loses during frying, ______" — so less moisture lost means fewer voids, so less oil absorbed, so less fat increase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the crispier the crust will be when frying is completed.': The passage does not link moisture loss to crispiness; it links moisture loss to oil absorption and fat content. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the less the fat content is increased through frying.': Less moisture lost means fewer voids, which means less oil fills in, which means less fat increase — a direct application of the passage's chain of cause and effect. ✓
  • Choice C — 'the slower the crust will lose its permeability.': The passage describes permeability as what allows moisture to keep moving from center to crust, but it does not connect the amount of moisture lost to the rate at which permeability is lost. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the higher the temperature must be to fry the food.': Temperature is never discussed in the passage; this is unsupported. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
